the source map string is standard for debugging.  \n- **Conclusion:** Overall, the code is benign; the minor risk score reflects cautiousness rather than actual malicious intent.\n\n---\n\n**Overall assessment:**\n\n- **Malware score:** All reports are consistent with no malware.  \n- **Obfuscated score:** Zero, as the code is clear and autogenerated.  \n- **Risk score:** Very low, as the code is standard, with the only slight increase in report 5 due to the embedded source map string, which is not inherently malicious.\n\n---\n\n**My own estimates after review:**\n\n- **Malware:** 0 — no signs of malicious activity.  \n- **Obfuscated:** 0 — code is straightforward and autogenerated.  \n- **Risk:** 0.1 — very minimal, mainly due to the embedded source map string, which is typical in transpiled code but could be flagged in some security reviews.\n\n**Final note:**  \nThe reports are consistent, and their scoring aligns with the code's nature. No evidence suggests any malicious intent or security flaw.
